name: Folded Pizza Sandwich
instructions:
1. Place the pizza dough in a medium bowl greased with cooking spray and cover lightly with plastic wrap. Let stand at room temperature until soft and pliable, about 1 hour. The dough should feel relaxed and easy to stretch.

2. While the dough rests, preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C) with a rack in the center position. Grate the Garlic finely and stir together with half of the Olive Oil in a small bowl to make garlic oil. In a separate bowl, whisk together the Red Wine Vinegar, Salt, and remaining Olive Oil to make a vinaigrette. Set both aside.

3. Turn the dough out onto a large sheet of parchment and cut into 2 equal pieces. Working with one piece at a time, stretch and roll the dough into a circle. Brush the dough generously with the garlic oil mixture, then fold the circle in half to create a half-moon shape. Brush the top lightly with more garlic oil. Place on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Repeat with the remaining dough and garlic oil.

4. Bake in the preheated oven until puffed and golden brown, 8 to 10 minutes. The bread should be crispy on the outside and soft inside. Let cool for about 3 minutes to make handling easier.

5. Toss the Baby Spinach with some of the vinaigrette in a medium bowl until lightly coated. Tear the Burrata into pieces. Gently open each pizza bread and place pieces of Burrata on the bottom half of each one.

6. Evenly stuff the dressed Baby Spinach, Mixed Cured Meats, and Sliced Pepperoncini into each pizza bread, distributing the fillings generously.

7. Drizzle with the remaining vinaigrette and Balsamic Glaze for a finishing touch. Serve immediately while the bread is still warm and the cheese is creamy.
simpleInstructions:
1. Let pizza dough sit at room temperature for 1 hour until soft and pliable.

2. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F and prepare garlic oil with Garlic and half the Olive Oil, plus vinaigrette with Red Wine Vinegar, Salt, and remaining Olive Oil.

3. Cut dough in half, form circles, brush with garlic oil, fold in half, and brush tops.

4. Bake for 8-10 minutes until puffed and golden, then cool briefly.

5. Toss Baby Spinach with some vinaigrette, tear Burrata, and place in opened pizza bread.

6. Stuff with spinach, Mixed Cured Meats, and Sliced Pepperoncini.

7. Drizzle with remaining vinaigrette and Balsamic Glaze, serve immediately.
